misc.title: #ONEPIECE1179 I agree with this Imu is the strongest, but he's also the loneliest When his subordinates can't do it, he just takes over Makes me wonder if we will get another storyline for Imu, other
misc.text: #ONEPIECE1179 I agree with this Imu is the strongest, but he's also the loneliest When his subordinates can't do it, he just takes over Makes me wonder if we will get another storyline for Imu, other than the Void Century, in an arc where the main theme is love WorstGenHQ (@WorstGenHQ) #ONEPIECE1179 I think Usopp is wrong about Imu. Imu is probably someone who has always fought alone, who has won his battles alone. Don't forget, he will be the opposite of Joyboy. Just look at the mural. Look at how the World Government is structured. Imu tries to gain his own subordinates through terror/power, but he will never have what Joyboy had: a real alliance, friends, and strong cohesion. And against this alliance, Imu fought "alone" (you can't count the few people at the bottom), against the entire population. Imu tries to do the same with his 20 kings, as well as Gorosei, Holy Knights, and CD. Building up his own "Alliance". But ultimately, he still comes out alone. It's possible that Usopp's words triggered him, and he insists on showing Usopp how he won the previous battles. Ultimately, Imu is a lone wolf, cruel and brutal. All the abilities of the HK or Gorosei are thanks to him, but he can revert them, just like Yhwach can... Usopp shouldn't have said that; he's awakened a giant disaster... And the fact that Nika and Nidhogg are there also triggered the move what Imu did this chapter. — https://nitter.net/WorstGenHQ/status/2039077902046707969#m

misc.text: #ONEPIECE1179 I think Usopp is wrong about Imu. Imu is probably someone who has always fought alone, who has won his battles alone. Don't forget, he will be the opposite of Joyboy. Just look at the mural. Look at how the World Government is structured. Imu tries to gain his own subordinates through terror/power, but he will never have what Joyboy had: a real alliance, friends, and strong cohesion. And against this alliance, Imu fought "alone" (you can't count the few people at the bottom), against the entire population. Imu tries to do the same with his 20 kings, as well as Gorosei, Holy Knights, and CD. Building up his own "Alliance". But ultimately, he still comes out alone. It's possible that Usopp's words triggered him, and he insists on showing Usopp how he won the previous battles. Ultimately, Imu is a lone wolf, cruel and brutal. All the abilities of the HK or Gorosei are thanks to him, but he can revert them, just like Yhwach can... Usopp shouldn't have said that; he's awakened a giant disaster... And the fact that Nika and Nidhogg are there also triggered the move what Imu did this chapter.
